title: Add WITH_PROMETHEUS flag for kind Quick Start
summary: Round-2 review of CC-0100 after addressing external reviewer berendt's review #1. The current diff (08cc262b..HEAD) addresses the BLOCKING/CRITICAL findings (B-001, C-001) by threading WITH_PROMETHEUS through hack/ci-deploy-operator.sh: the script now reads the optional env var, builds a helm_args array, and conditionally appends --set monitoring.serviceMonitor.enabled=true; the e2e-prometheus job exports WITH_PROMETHEUS=true on the Deploy operator step. Warning-level fixes: removed the leftover 'Reviewer: please verify' comment in hack/deploy-infra.sh:626 (W-001); added Makefile target stage-prometheus-dashboard with documented prerequisite for kustomize-build flows in docs/reference/infrastructure/infrastructure-manifests.md (W-002); updated docs/reference/ci-cd/ci-workflow.md with the new e2e-prometheus and shell-unit-tests-cc-0100 jobs, bumped count 22→24, switched 'four E2E jobs' to 'five E2E jobs' (W-003); chainsaw suite gained Step 1b (HelmRelease values render the kind tuning, alertmanager/node-exporter/kube-state-metrics absent, servicemonitors CRD owned by prometheus-operator-crds — REQ-002) and Step 4b (sha256 byte-identical dashboard ConfigMap vs canonical source — REQ-004); netshoot probe pods pinned by digest (docker.io/nicolaka/netshoot:v0.13@sha256:a20c2531…) at all four call sites. Three new review patterns persist the lessons: audit-suspended-helmrelease-patches-for-inert-overrides, check-chart-default-values-match-e2e-job-expectations, verify-feature-flags-propagate-end-to-end-through-deployment-chain. The fixes are minimal, mechanical, and consistent with established patterns (CI-script env-var bridge, helm_args array idiom). No new bugs introduced; the in-pod sha256 check correctly relies on jsonpath emitting raw value bytes (configMapGenerator preserves trailing newline so source/CM hashes match), and the kind-posture grep regex correctly detects the three excluded workload kinds without false-matching the Prometheus StatefulSet.

tests_checklist.item: Step 1b chainsaw assertion verifies HelmRelease.spec.values renders crds/alertmanager/nodeExporter/kubeStateMetrics disabled and retention=6h
Step 1b chainsaw assertion verifies alertmanager/node-exporter/kube-state-metrics workloads are absent in the monitoring namespace
Step 1b chainsaw assertion verifies the servicemonitors.monitoring.coreos.com CRD is owned by prometheus-operator-crds (no flap with kube-prometheus-stack)
Step 4b chainsaw assertion verifies the keystone-operator-dashboard ConfigMap content is sha256 byte-identical to operators/keystone/dashboards/keystone-operator.json
Per-step monitoring-namespace presence guard preserved across new Step 1b and Step 4b for default-safe behavior
netshoot probe images pinned by digest at all four kubectl run call sites (lines 338, 371, 422, 454)
Worst-case loop wall-clock fits inside per-step chainsaw timeouts (Step 1b/4b each 60s budget vs <2s expected)

Step 4b sha256 check accounts for jsonpath emitting raw bytes including trailing newline (kustomize configMapGenerator preserves the file's trailing newline, sha256sum sees identical input on both sides)

code_quality_checklist.item: ci-deploy-operator.sh helm_args array follows shellcheck-clean array-expansion idiom ("${helm_args[@]}" preserves quoting, no word-splitting)
ci-deploy-operator.sh WITH_PROMETHEUS default uses ${WITH_PROMETHEUS:-false} consistent with the WITH_CHAOS_MESH precedent in deploy-infra.sh:65-69
Strict equality `[[ "${WITH_PROMETHEUS}" == "true" ]]` rejects accidental yes/1/True values (mirrors deploy-infra.sh)
CC-0100 + REQ-IDs cited in every new comment block (ci-deploy-operator.sh, ci.yaml step env, Makefile, infrastructure-manifests.md, chainsaw Step 1b/4b headers)
DECISION block in deploy-infra.sh:612-625 rewritten as stable rationale; the leftover 'Reviewer: please verify' line is gone
Three new review patterns added under .planwerk/review_patterns/ documenting the suspended-HelmRelease/chart-default/feature-flag-propagation lessons for future reviews

security_checklist.item: WITH_PROMETHEUS env var is a boolean toggle — no shell-injection surface in helm_args or ci.yaml step env
Helm --set value is a static literal `monitoring.serviceMonitor.enabled=true` — no user-controlled interpolation
netshoot probe image now pinned by sha256 digest — protects against upstream tag retargeting (resolves the prior review's MINOR S3 / I-001)
Step 1b/4b scripts read kubectl output into shell vars without exec/eval — no command injection
Step 4b sha256 comparison uses fixed repo-relative source path (operators/keystone/dashboards/keystone-operator.json) — no path traversal

architecture_checklist.item: Install-time helm --set is the single source of truth for ServiceMonitor enablement on the kind path; the kubectl patch on the suspended Flux HelmRelease remains durable but is no longer load-bearing for the e2e-prometheus suite
Production overlay (deploy/flux-system/) untouched — kind-only opt-in posture preserved

Three new review patterns address structural classes of bugs (suspended-HelmRelease patches, chart-default-vs-e2e-expectation mismatch, end-to-end feature-flag propagation) — generalisable beyond CC-0100
Step 1b CRD ownership assertion locks in the design contract that prometheus-operator-crds is the sole owner of monitoring.coreos.com/v1 CRDs (no dual-owner reconcile flap)

dry_yagni_checklist.item: Makefile stage-prometheus-dashboard target performs the same cp -f as deploy-infra.sh (no logic duplication; both call the same canonical path)
Step 4b sha256 check is content-addressed (no need to enumerate dashboard fields)
ci-deploy-operator.sh helm_args array is the minimal change vs the previous flat --set chain

fail_fast_checklist.item: Step 1b jq -e exits non-zero on predicate failure, surfaces immediately under set -e
Step 1b workload-absence check exits 1 explicitly with diagnostic message on regression
Step 4b sha256 mismatch prints both hashes and exits 1 — diagnostic-rich failure
ci-deploy-operator.sh fails fast on missing OPERATOR/IMAGE_REPO via :? expansion (unchanged)

defensive_checklist.item: Step 1b CRD-ownership kubectl get tolerates absent CRD via 2>/dev/null || echo "" — empty owner triggers the explicit FAIL message rather than a cryptic kubectl error
Step 4b SKIP guard at top of script (kubectl get ns monitoring) keeps the test default-safe on clusters without WITH_PROMETHEUS

ci-deploy-operator.sh does not set --set monitoring.serviceMonitor.enabled=false explicitly — relies on chart default to remain false (preserves the conservative chart default for non-kind chart consumers)

suggested_improvements: Optional polish: the kind-posture grep regex in chainsaw Step 1b uses a single-character class `(...|...|...)` — splitting into three explicit kubectl get -o name calls (one per excluded workload) would yield clearer failure diagnostics, but the current form is correct and the catch block already dumps the full namespace contents for triage.
Optional polish: ci-deploy-operator.sh could log which value of WITH_PROMETHEUS it observed at the top of step 2, mirroring deploy-infra.sh's banner line, for symmetric CI-log debuggability.
next_steps: Merge: review #1 BLOCKING/CRITICAL items (B-001, C-001) plus all five WARNING items targeting test gaps (W-001 helmrelease values, W-002 workload absence, W-003 CRD ownership, W-004 dashboard byte equality) and the docs gaps (W-001 stale comment, W-002 kustomize prerequisite, W-003 ci-workflow.md) are addressed.
After merge: monitor the e2e-prometheus job's first cold-cache CI run to confirm the digest-pinned netshoot image pulls within the worst-case 30s pod-startup margin baked into Step 3/4 timeouts.
